acceptance_criteria: docs/GATE-M1.md exists and records Gate ID, main commit, task status evidence for SIM-001 through SIM-006, reviewer thread IDs, handoff paths, CI evidence, local validation commands, known risks, and a PASS/BLOCK conclusion.

M1 acceptance matrix evidence is checked against docs/11-roadmap.md and docs/23-milestone-acceptance-matrix.md, including command semantics, deterministic 3650-day run, save v1, minimal read model, invariant/golden/protocol tests, corrupt save rejection, and key API tests.
Any gap between Roadmap wording and actual evidence is explicitly classified as PASS_WITH_INTERPRETATION, FOLLOW_UP_REQUIRED, or BLOCKED; no evidence is invented.
M1 exit does not change frozen product/platform/core architecture decisions, does not introduce implementation code, and does not start M2 implementation work.
systems_architect produces a validated gate writer handoff and independent qa_reviewer produces a validated ACCEPT handoff before lead marks the gate accepted.
If the gate passes, project state may advance current_milestone from M1 to M2 only after PR/CI/merge; M5 remains a Human Gate.
required_tests: node -e JSON validation for gate task, goal-mode-state, model-routing-state, and relevant handoffs
notes: MILESTONE-AUTONOMY-AMENDMENT-001 allows ordinary M1-M4 milestone exits to pass autonomously only when the acceptance matrix is satisfied, CI and validation pass, independent reviewers ACCEPT, and no R4/frozen-decision Human Gate is active.
Roadmap M1 says Chrome/Node/Electron hash consistency; verify whether existing Worker/Web-compatible hash plus Windows Desktop Smoke is sufficient or whether this should become a follow-up/blocker.
